message: br-bd-keihq add btrfs renameat2 flag rejection coverage

files.patch:
@@ -754,7 +754,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-k9zm","title":"Apply fast commit operations to filesystem metadata during mount","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 fast commit (FC) is a lightweight journal mechanism that records individual filesystem operations (create, link, unlink, add_range, del_range, inode update) instead of full block snapshots. On mount, these operations are replayed to recover metadata state that wasn't flushed to the main journal.

CURRENT STATE: The replay_fast_commit() function in ffs-journal parses FC tag streams and extracts FcOperation variants (Create, Link, Unlink, AddRange, DelRange, Inode). The mount-time path (maybe_collect_ext4_fast_commit_evidence in ffs-core) reads these operations and stores them as Ext4FastCommitReplayEvidence — purely observational.

FEATURE_PARITY.md honestly states: 'FrankenFS still relies on full JBD2 replay for authoritative metadata recovery; FC operations are observed and reported, not yet applied to filesystem state during mount.'

WHAT NEEDS TO HAPPEN:
1. After full JBD2 replay, check if the journal has FC blocks (s_num_fc_blocks > 0 in JBD2 superblock).
2. Parse the FC tag stream from the journal's FC tail region.
3. For each committed FC transaction (HEAD...TAIL pair):
   a. Create: allocate inode, add dir entry to parent.
   b. Link: add dir entry to parent (inode already exists).
   c. Unlink: remove dir entry from parent.
   d. AddRange: insert extent mapping for the inode.
   e. DelRange: remove extent mapping range.
   f. Inode: update inode metadata (size, timestamps, etc.).
4. Write the updated metadata blocks to disk.
5. Clear the FC area in the journal superblock.

COMPLEXITY: High. This requires wiring FC operations into the same mutation paths used by the FUSE write layer (ext4_create, ext4_add_dir_entry, ext4_unlink, extent allocation, inode update). The FC operations are at a higher level of abstraction than JBD2 block replay.

KEY RISKS:
- FC operations may reference inodes that don't exist yet (the create hadn't been flushed). Need to handle this gracefully.
- FC operations may conflict with JBD2 replay results (JBD2 replayed a partial state, FC completes it). Need idempotent application.
- Truncated FC streams must fall back to full JBD2 replay only (the existing fallback behavior is correct).

DEPENDENCIES:
- Requires the fast commit dentry parsing fix (commit 6e241b0) which corrected the ext4_fc_dentry_info struct layout.
- Should be done after i_version support (bd-pzc6) since FC inode updates may need to bump version.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A test creates a synthetic image with a committed FC transaction (HEAD + Create + TAIL), mounts it, and verifies the directory entry exists.
- A test with a truncated FC stream (no TAIL) falls back to JBD2-only replay.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to remove the 'observed and reported, not yet applied' caveat.
- Evidence logging records which FC operations were applied.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:17.638537685Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:49:44.306396946Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:49:44.305935052Z","close_reason":"FC operations now processed at mount time via apply_fast_commit_operations(). Committed transactions are logged as observational evidence complementing JBD2 replay. InodeUpdate triggers verification read. Fallback to JBD2-only on truncated streams. FEATURE_PARITY.md caveat removed. Commit 6a4dd9c.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fast_commit","journal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k9zm","depends_on_id":"bd-pzc6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:38.088785279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-kdk","title":"btrfs: Implement single-device logical->physical mapping (sys_chunk only)","description":"Goal: given a logical bytenr, map it to (device_id, physical_bytenr) using only the sys_chunk_array bootstrap data.

Scope (v1):
- single-device images only
- only mapping via sys_chunk_array (enough to read the chunk tree/root tree)

Deliverables:
- Parse chunk items from sys_chunk_array.
- Implement map(logical) -> physical (with bounds checking).

Acceptance:
- Fixture test: pick known logical addresses (superblock.root, superblock.chunk_root) and verify mapping lands inside the device.
- No panics; invalid mapping returns ParseError/Format.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:42.802564278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:06:19.809203889Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:06:19.809180495Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kdk","depends_on_id":"bd-2vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:11.407714905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ke0s9","title":"Freeze exact extent mapping diagnostics contract in ffs-extent","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:15:42.685861307Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:21:40.972775972Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T23:21:40.970725831Z","close_reason":"Added an exact golden contract for representative extent mappings in ffs-extent; verified with focused rch test plus workspace check, clippy, and fmt","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing-golden-artifacts"]}
-{"id":"bd-keihq","title":"add mounted-path btrfs renameat2 flag-rejection coverage for NOREPLACE and EXCHANGE","description":"Survey finding: the shared ffs-fuse rename handler rejects any nonzero rename flags with EINVAL before dispatch, but btrfs mounted-path tests only exercise plain rename success. Scope: add mounted-path btrfs probes that invoke renameat2 with RENAME_NOREPLACE and RENAME_EXCHANGE (or another representative nonzero flag set) via libc/ctypes, assert exact EINVAL, and verify source/destination names and contents remain unchanged. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes the public rename flag-rejection contract.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:45:57.453626381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:45:57.453626381Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-keihq","title":"add mounted-path btrfs renameat2 flag-rejection coverage for NOREPLACE and EXCHANGE","description":"Survey finding: the shared ffs-fuse rename handler rejects any nonzero rename flags with EINVAL before dispatch, but btrfs mounted-path tests only exercise plain rename success. Scope: add mounted-path btrfs probes that invoke renameat2 with RENAME_NOREPLACE and RENAME_EXCHANGE (or another representative nonzero flag set) via libc/ctypes, assert exact EINVAL, and verify source/destination names and contents remain unchanged. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes the public rename flag-rejection contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:45:57.453626381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:51:23.523067176Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:51:23.523067176Z","close_reason":"Added mounted-path btrfs renameat2 unsupported-flag EINVAL coverage for RENAME_NOREPLACE and RENAME_EXCHANGE; verified via focused rch test plus fmt/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-kfc4","title":"Systematic documentation sweep: align README, spec, and code comments with 90/90 parity state","description":"PROBLEM: Multiple documentation sources contain stale information that conflicts with the current 90/90 feature parity state:

1. README.md line ~1148-1153 (Security Model section): Still says 'Required feature flags: FILETYPE + EXTENTS' and 'Excluded feature flags: ENCRYPT, CASEFOLD, INLINE_DATA'. All of these are now accepted. EXTENTS is no longer required.

2. README.md line ~1305-1310 (Feature Parity section): Shows 75/75 parity counts. Should be 90/90.

3. README.md line ~19 (badge): Shows '2146 passing' tests. Actual count is 3,591+.

4. README.md line ~18 (parity badge): Shows 100% but based on 75/75.

5. COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md: May have stale references to COMPRESSION and JOURNAL_DEV as rejected features (was partially updated but may have other instances).

6. Code comments in ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs line ~160: The REJECTED_V1 comment still mentions 'legacy ext2 dead format' for COMPRESSION — we now fully support e2compr R/W.

7. ffs-core test names: Some test names like 'open_fs_rejects_standalone_journal_device' are correct but nearby comments may reference old behavior.

8. The compute_bitmap_checksum function (XOR-based, wrong) at ffs-ondisk line 1743 coexists with the correct ext4_bitmap_checksum function (chain-based). The wrong function should be removed or clearly deprecated.

SCOPE: This is a documentation-only task with no functional code changes. It should be quick but thorough.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All README.md parity numbers, test counts, and feature exclusion lists are current.
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC feature tables match FEATURE_PARITY.md.
- No code comments reference COMPRESSION or JOURNAL_DEV as 'rejected' or 'dead'.
- The wrong compute_bitmap_checksum function is either removed (if unused) or clearly marked.
- A grep for 'rejected' in code comments finds only legitimate uses.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:50.542198901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:36:14.007336035Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:36:14.006861707Z","close_reason":"Updated README parity (90/90), test counts (3591+), feature scope (all incompat accepted), security model (FILETYPE only required). Commit 5537150.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","docs"]}
 {"id":"bd-ki29","title":"Add bitmap checksums, FLEX_BG support, SPARSE_SUPER2 awareness to ext4 metadata","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-21T20:16:04.593622605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T20:16:18.304002457Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T20:16:18.303331377Z","close_reason":"Implemented 5 parity improvements:
(1) Added block_bitmap_csum and inode_bitmap_csum fields to Ext4GroupDesc with full parse/serialize at offsets 0x18/0x1A (32-byte) and 0x38/0x3A (64-byte extension)
(2) Added compute_bitmap_checksum() and verify_bitmap_checksum() functions for ext4 bitmap CRC32C validation (seed XOR group)
(3) Added stamp_block/inode_bitmap_checksum() to write checksums into group descriptors (removed duplicates with PearlBadger's work)
(4) FLEX_BG support in reserved_blocks_in_group(): now marks OTHER groups' metadata residing in this group as reserved, preventing allocator from corrupting foreign metadata
(5) Added backup_superblock_groups(group_count) method to Ext4Superblock for SPARSE_SUPER2-aware backup location enumeration
MMP parsing already implemented by PearlBadger. 1796 tests pass across 10 leaf crates (ffs-journal has 5 pre-existing failures from another agent).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alloc","checksum","ondisk","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-kkh1","title":"Add JBD2 commit checksum field-zeroing metamorphic coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:47:39.708143632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:53:38.583418829Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:53:38.581014618Z","close_reason":"Added metamorphic coverage proving JBD2 commit checksum stamping is invariant to preexisting checksum-field bytes; validated with rch cargo test -p ffs-journal field_invariant -- --nocapture, rch cargo check -p ffs-journal --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p ffs-journal --all-targets -- -D warnings, and rch cargo fmt --check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -105,6 +105,9 @@ visible workspace-entry or parent-file drift.
 Mounted-path ext4 `renameat2` flag-rejection coverage now also freezes exact
 `EINVAL` for `RENAME_NOREPLACE` and `RENAME_EXCHANGE`, with no source,
 destination, or root-entry drift.
+Mounted-path btrfs `renameat2` flag-rejection coverage now also freezes exact
+`EINVAL` for `RENAME_NOREPLACE` and `RENAME_EXCHANGE`, with no source,
+destination, or workspace-entry drift.
 Mounted-path ext4 read-only `fallocate` coverage now also freezes exact `EROFS`
 for representative preallocate and punch-hole mutation attempts, with no
 file-byte or apparent-size drift.
